Example: {"type":"closing","bg":"ink","eyebrow":"NEXT STEPS","title":"One Convention, one standard","lede":"Owner: Policy · Review: end-Q1"}. Mirrors the cover in inverse — defaults to the dark `ink` poster. Use to state the ask, not to thank the audience. RA brand-mark + wordmark drawn automatically.

Final slide; thank-you / next-steps / CTA. One of the dark (ink) slides that sandwich the deck (open dark cover, close dark closing). Title is Source Serif 4 (display) and short (<=8 words).

Closing slide. Like the cover, anchors on cobalt (bg:signal) or dark ink poster (bg:ink), gets the brand-mark + wordmark and the auto-drawn orb. Closing title may be centered. On dark bg the eyebrow + dash are amber.

Closing / thank-you slide. In template mode routes to layout RA_thankyou: renderer fills idx 11 = title and idx 1 = subtitle; the layout supplies a near-black fill, the RA logo lockup top-left, an amber geomark bottom-right, and the page number. Uses the top-left logo lockup with no footer lockup (hero treatment).

props.when_to_use: Final slide; thank-you / next-steps / CTA

nodes.name: column
nodes.content: Vertical bars. Use for ranking N items where N<=7 (sort descending, lead series in cobalt) OR for two metrics over the same categories (column with two series). Native editable PowerPoint chart with data labels.

props.when_to_use: Ranking N<=7 items (sort descending); or two metrics over same categories (two series)

nodes.name: deck-rule-use-compose-deck-not-python
nodes.content: Hard Rule 1: Use the compose_deck tool — NOT run_python_code — for any deck output. The run_python_code container has no UC volume mount, so writes to /Volumes go to tmpfs and are silently lost; compose_deck uploads through the Files API so bytes actually land in Unity Catalog.

props.statement: Use compose_deck — not run_python_code — for any deck output.

nodes.name: deck-rule-cover-plus-content
nodes.content: Hard Rule 2: Every deck has a cover slide + at least one content slide.

props.statement: Every deck has a cover + at least one content slide.

nodes.name: deck-rule-never-fabricate-urls
nodes.content: Hard Rule 3: Never fabricate URLs. Quote the tool's returned preview_url and pptx_url verbatim. Always surface BOTH URLs to the user.

props.statement: Never fabricate URLs. Quote the tool's return (both URLs) verbatim.

nodes.name: deck-rule-count-slides-honestly
nodes.content: Hard Rule 4: Count slides honestly. Report the tool's returned slide_count to the user.

props.statement: Count slides honestly. Report slide_count to the user.

nodes.name: deck-rule-no-placeholder-content
nodes.content: Hard Rule 5: No placeholder content. Never ship 'TBD', 'Lorem ipsum', or 'Insert content here'.

props.statement: No placeholder content ('TBD', 'Lorem ipsum', 'Insert content here').

nodes.name: deck-rule-stop-querying-inline-format
nodes.content: Hard Rule 5a: Stop querying once you have the numbers; inline-format them in the spec. After a query returns the raw values, do NOT chain more queries to pre-format into K/M/B strings or rounded decimals — write the formatted string directly (e.g. 'value':'17', 'unit':'x lower'). One or two queries per deck is normal; ten queries means over-engineering.

props.statement: Stop querying once you have the numbers. Inline-format them in the spec; do not chain formatting queries.

nodes.name: deck-rule-short-titles
nodes.content: Hard Rule 5b: Cover + closing + section titles must be short (<=8 words, fits two lines). Push period/scope/preparer detail into `lede`. Example: title 'The Care Lottery' + lede 'Why a district's access to healthcare varies up to 80x across India.' — not a period crammed into the title.

props.statement: Cover + closing + section titles <=8 words; push detail into lede.

nodes.name: deck-rule-build-anyway-on-missing-data
nodes.content: Hard Rule 6: If data is unavailable, build the deck anyway with the closest available data. After one attempt that confirms absence, pivot. The first content slide MUST then be a data_note naming the requested vs substituted scope and the reason. Reaching the loop guard without ever calling compose_deck is a worse failure than admitting a gap.

props.statement: Data unavailable -> build the deck anyway with closest available data; first content slide is a data_note.

nodes.name: deck-rule-no-retry-on-render-failed
nodes.content: Hard Rule 7: Don't retry on render_failed with the same spec. Strip the offending slide (the error message names the slide index) and re-call. Never retry the same call twice with identical args; two retries max per turn.

props.statement: Don't retry render_failed with the same spec — strip the offending slide and re-call.

nodes.name: deck-rule-every-slide-needs-a-visual
nodes.content: Every slide needs a visual element — chart, big number, KPI grid, brand-mark, or shape. No text-only slides; they are forgettable.

props.statement: Every slide needs a visual element (chart, big number, KPI grid, brand-mark, or shape). No text-only slides.

nodes.name: deck-rule-vary-layouts
nodes.content: Use varied layouts — never repeat the same slide layout. Vary stat_callout, chart, kpi_grid, two_column, quote across the deck.

props.statement: Use varied layouts; never repeat the same layout across the deck.

nodes.name: deck-rule-sandwich-dark-light
nodes.content: Sandwich dark + light: open with an ink cover (cobalt orb), close with an ink closing; section dividers can go signal (cobalt). Content slides stay light (paper / cream).

props.statement: Sandwich dark + light: dark cover + dark closing; signal section dividers; light content slides.

nodes.name: deck-rule-one-display-number-per-slide
nodes.content: Don't put more than one display-size number per slide — pick ONE headline (stat_callout); everything else is a kpi_grid or chart.

props.statement: One display-size number per slide; one headline stat_callout, rest in kpi_grid/chart.

nodes.name: deck-rule-title-says-the-data
nodes.content: Don't over-explain the chart in the title. The title says what the data says ('Bihar records the fewest facilities'), not the chart kind ('Bar chart of facility coverage').

props.statement: Chart title states the data's message, not the chart type.

nodes.name: deck-rule-qa-after-compose
nodes.content: QA (required) after every compose_deck call: assume there are problems and find them. (1) Re-read your deck_spec for empty strings, 'TBD', numbers that don't match source data, captions contradicting the chart, missing periods. (2) Mention the preview_url so the user can spot-check before downloading. (3) If anything looks off, build a corrected spec and call compose_deck again (id is keyed on title+spec so a new spec gets a new id). Stay within the iteration budget — every avoidable read_file is one fewer round for data / the compose call.

props.statement: After every compose_deck call: re-read the spec for errors, mention preview_url, re-compose a corrected spec if anything is off.

nodes.name: deck-rule-authoring-rhythm
nodes.content: Authoring rhythm: (1) Pull data (Genie/SQL/stored DFs); know the headline number. (2) Plan content — outline 4-8 slides. (3) If first deck this session read spec_reference.md; if non-default visual brief also read design.md. (4) Compose the full deck_spec list. (5) Call compose_deck(title=..., deck_spec=[...]) once. (6) Surface both URLs. Most prompts only need spec_reference.md once per session.

props.statement: Rhythm: pull data -> plan 4-8 slides -> read spec_reference.md (first deck) -> compose deck_spec -> call once -> surface both URLs.

nodes.name: ra-deck-signal-is-the-spine
nodes.content: Signal cobalt #254BB2 is the spine of every RA deck — dominant on covers, section dividers, table headers, and the lead chart series. If a secondary colour (amber #DF9B44 / cyan #2695AC / magenta #913F82) starts to dominate, pull it back. Neutrals: cool-slate ink (#050A0F text -> #F9FAFC paper).

props.statement: Signal cobalt #254BB2 is the spine: covers, section dividers, table headers, lead chart series. Keep accents (amber/cyan/magenta) secondary.

nodes.name: ra-deck-serif-display-only
nodes.content: Type: Manrope (sans — UI, body, content-slide titles) + Source Serif 4 (display — cover hero, section titles, the one hero number / stat_callout, KPI values, pull-quotes) + JetBrains Mono (page numbers, small data). Serif is the editorial signature — use it ONLY for display moments. Content-slide titles and all body text are Manrope.

props.statement: Source Serif 4 for display moments only (cover hero, section title, hero stat, KPI values, pull-quotes); Manrope for content titles + body; JetBrains Mono for page numbers/small data.

nodes.name: ra-deck-chrome-motif
nodes.content: Commit to the chrome motif across every slide: the eyebrow + dash ABOVE the title, the cobalt accent tick, and the brand-mark + page number. They ARE the brand presence — you don't need a logo on every slide.

props.statement: Carry the chrome motif (eyebrow + dash above title, cobalt accent tick, brand-mark + page number) across every slide.

nodes.name: ra-deck-no-accent-line-under-titles
nodes.content: NEVER use accent lines under titles — the eyebrow + dash ABOVE the title IS the one accent line. Lines under titles are the hallmark of AI-generated decks.

props.statement: Never put accent lines under titles; the eyebrow + dash above the title is the only accent line.

nodes.name: ra-deck-no-logo-field
nodes.content: No raster logos and no `logo` field. The RA brand-mark (a cobalt rounded square + a small cross-hair) and the 'Resonance Analytics' wordmark are drawn natively onto cover / section / closing slides by the renderer. Don't add a logo.

props.statement: No raster logos / no `logo` field — brand-mark + wordmark are drawn natively by the renderer.

nodes.name: ra-deck-left-align-body
nodes.content: Don't center body text — left-align lists. Center only big-stat values, pull-quotes, and cover/closing titles.

props.statement: Left-align body/lists; center only big-stat values, pull-quotes, and cover/closing titles.

nodes.name: ra-deck-max-6-series
nodes.content: <=6 series per chart. Beyond that the palette runs out — split into multiple charts or use a stacked treatment.

props.statement: <=6 series per chart; beyond that split charts or use a stacked treatment.

nodes.name: ra-deck-fonts-install-for-pptx
nodes.content: Fonts: Manrope + Source Serif 4 are referenced by name. The HTML preview loads them from Google Fonts and is always pixel-faithful. In PowerPoint they render faithfully IF installed on the viewer's machine; otherwise PowerPoint substitutes, preserving the sans/serif contrast. For board-ready exports, install both fonts (free, Google Fonts) before exporting to PDF.

props.statement: Install Manrope + Source Serif 4 (Google Fonts) for faithful PPTX/PDF rendering; HTML preview is always faithful.

nodes.name: compose_document
nodes.content:
compose_document owns binary office docs: docx / xlsx / csv / pdf. It uploads to the same documents/ volume folder as compose_deck. Office-doc generation must go through compose_document, never run_python_code (UC tmpfs trap, trace tr-83d05…). Presentation decks are split OUT to compose_deck; compose_document keeps everything else.

Sibling document tool. After compose_deck took over the *pptx* presentation path, compose_document still handles docx / xlsx / csv / pdf. For anything that is 'slides', use compose_deck instead.

props.note: never use run_python_code for office docs (UC tmpfs trap)

nodes.name: common-slide-fields
nodes.content: Common fields on EVERY slide: `type` (str, required — one of the slide types). `eyebrow` (str, default "" — short label above the title, uppercase + dash, e.g. "FINDINGS · 02"; renders cobalt on light backgrounds, amber on dark). `bg` (str, default "paper" — "paper" white / "cream" warm off-white / "signal" cobalt with white text / "ink" near-black poster with white text; aliases "red"->signal, "charcoal"/"dark"->ink accepted). `footer_note` (str, default "" — bottom-left small text; the deck page number is always on the right). `motif` (bool, default true — on cover/closing with a dark ink background, draws the cobalt orb; set false to suppress).

props.statement: Every slide accepts type (required), eyebrow, bg (paper/cream/signal/ink), footer_note, and motif. Background aliases red->signal, charcoal/dark->ink.

nodes.name: no-logo-field
nodes.content: There is no `logo` field. The Resonance Analytics brand-mark (a cobalt rounded square with a small cross-hair) + the "Resonance Analytics" wordmark are drawn automatically on `cover`, `section_divider`, and `closing` slides; a small brand-mark sits next to the page number on content slides. You don't reference it in the spec and must not add a `logo` key.

props.statement: Never add a `logo` field. The RA brand-mark + wordmark are auto-drawn on cover/section_divider/closing; a small mark sits by the page number on content slides.

nodes.name: caption-required-on-every-data-slide
nodes.content: Required: every stat_callout, chart, chart_commentary, kpi_grid, and table MUST have a `caption` (or, for kpi_grid, a meaningful title + eyebrow). The number alone is not the insight. The caption names: scope, period, and one interpretation. Bad: "caption":"Facilities by state" (re-states the title). Good: "caption":"Bihar records the fewest facilities in the ~10k sample despite the highest health burden — coverage, not census."

props.statement: Every stat_callout/chart/chart_commentary/kpi_grid/table needs a caption naming scope + period + one interpretation. Don't restate the title; state the insight.

nodes.name: chart-palette-and-sort-rules
nodes.content: Chart palette ordering (assigned in series order): 1. 254BB2 cobalt Signal (lead), 2. DF9B44 amber, 3. 2695AC cyan, 4. 913F82 magenta, 5. 85B0FF light cobalt, 6. 52657A slate (neutral/"rest"). Sort rules: Column/bar — sort categories by value descending unless they have a natural order (months, regions). Pie — largest segment at 12 o'clock, clockwise descending; group anything under 5% into "Other". Line — chronological, don't sort by value.

props.statement: Chart series colours assigned in fixed order (cobalt, amber, cyan, magenta, light cobalt, slate). Column/bar sort by value desc (unless natural order); pie largest at 12 o'clock clockwise, <5%->Other; line chronological.

nodes.name: sanity-checklist
nodes.content: Sanity checklist before calling compose_deck: (1) Every slide has a `type` from the catalogue. (2) No value/title/caption is an empty string. (3) No "Lorem ipsum"/"TBD"/"Insert content here". (4) Cobalt Signal used for emphasis; accents (amber/cyan/magenta) stay supporting. (5) Each chart's series[*].values length matches categories length. (6) Each chart has a caption. (7) Cover + >=1 content + closing (closing recommended, not required). (8) If scope/period was substituted, the SECOND slide is a data_note. (9) No `logo` field anywhere (the brand-mark is automatic).

props.statement: Pre-call checklist: typed slides, no empty/placeholder text, cobalt for emphasis only, chart values length == categories, captions on charts, cover+content(+closing), data_note as 2nd slide if scope substituted, no logo field.

nodes.name: ra-cobalt-signal-palette
nodes.content: Resonance Analytics palette: cobalt 254BB2 "Signal" is the lead/emphasis colour (used for the stat_callout ~130pt hero value, kpi tile accent ticks, table header rows, the chart lead series, eyebrows on light backgrounds, commentary labels). Supporting accents stay subordinate: DF9B44 amber, 2695AC cyan, 913F82 magenta, 85B0FF light cobalt, 52657A slate (neutral/rest). On dark (ink) backgrounds eyebrows and commentary labels render amber instead of cobalt. Backgrounds: paper (white), cream (warm off-white), signal (cobalt, white text), ink (near-black poster, white text — the RA signature with cobalt orb).

props.statement: Cobalt 254BB2 'Signal' is the single emphasis colour; amber/cyan/magenta/light-cobalt/slate are supporting accents. Eyebrows/labels switch cobalt->amber on dark backgrounds.

nodes.name: ra-typography
nodes.content: Resonance Analytics typography: Source Serif 4 display for hero/emphatic text — cover title ~72pt, stat_callout value ~130pt (cobalt Signal), kpi tile values, quote text in Source Serif 4 italic ~40pt. Eyebrows are uppercase + dash short labels.

props.statement: Source Serif 4 (display + italic) for hero text: cover title ~72pt, stat value ~130pt, quote ~40pt italic. Eyebrows uppercase + dash.

nodes.name: ra-dark-bg-auto-contrast
nodes.content: On a dark (ink) background the chart axes + legend auto-switch to white, and eyebrows + chart_commentary labels render amber instead of cobalt. The cobalt orb motif draws on cover/closing ink backgrounds (suppressible via motif:false). Logos auto-adapt — the brand-mark is drawn automatically.

props.statement: Dark (ink) backgrounds auto-switch chart axes/legend to white and eyebrows/commentary labels to amber; cobalt-orb motif draws on cover/closing ink unless motif:false.

nodes.name: deck_template.pptx
nodes.content: PPTX template chrome auto-loaded by the compose-pptx renderer (template mode). Referenced by the compose_deck tool, not parsed in the spec.

props.purpose: PowerPoint template chrome the renderer paints content on top of (RA brand-mark, wordmark, page numbers)

nodes.name: RA brand palette — Signal cobalt spine
nodes.content: Resonance Analytics 'Boardroom Editorial' design system. PRIMARY is Signal cobalt-indigo #254BB2 (--signal-600) — the brand spine, dominant on covers, dividers, table headers, and the lead chart series. Neutrals are 'ink' cool-slate: #050A0F text (--ink-900) on #F9FAFC paper (--ink-50). Surfaces are white #FFFFFF and warm cream #FBF0E4. The full Signal scale: 50 #F0F5FF, 100 #DAE9FF, 200 #B5D2FF, 300 #85B0FF (light cobalt / chart accent 5), 400 #5080EB, 500 #345FCF (orb highlight), 600 #254BB2 (PRIMARY), 700 #17368D (solid cobalt background bg:signal), 800 #0A2163, 900 #051139. Full Ink scale: 50 #F9FAFC paper, 100 #EFF2F5 surface-2/zebra, 200 #E1E5E9 border, 300 #C6CBD0 muted-on-dark, 400 #9A9FA5, 500 #6D7277 soft text/captions, 600 #494E52 muted text, 700 #2F3338, 800 #161B20, 900 #050A0F body text, 950 #010204 dark poster background (bg:ink). Tokens authored in oklch; these hex are the sRGB renderings used by the PPTX renderer.

props.statement: Signal cobalt #254BB2 is the brand primary/spine; ink cool-slate neutrals (#050A0F→#F9FAFC); white + warm cream #FBF0E4 surfaces.

nodes.name: RA accent palette — gold/teal/plum/slate/sand
nodes.content: Accent (chart + infographic) palette, harmonized with the cobalt primary: --gold #DF9B44 amber (chart accent 1 · eyebrow on dark backgrounds), --teal #2695AC cyan (chart accent 2), --plum #913F82 magenta (chart accent 3), --slate #52657A neutral (chart 'rest'), --sand #BDD5E6 tints. Use accents sparingly; never let an accent dominate a composition — pull it back to cobalt or ink. Status colours: --success #14874E green (delta up), --warning #D79628 amber, --danger #BA2B2E red (delta down).

props.statement: Accents gold #DF9B44, teal #2695AC, plum #913F82, slate #52657A, sand #BDD5E6 — used sparingly, subordinate to cobalt/ink. Status: success #14874E, warning #D79628, danger #BA2B2E.

nodes.name: RA typography — Source Serif 4 display + Manrope body
nodes.content: Display type is Source Serif 4 (serif) — covers, hero numbers, section openers, pull-quotes. Body/UI type is Manrope (sans) — titles, body, labels, UI. Mono is JetBrains Mono — page numbers, small data labels. Type scale: display-xl 128px Source Serif 400 (cover hero only); display-l 88px Source Serif 400 (big stat, large cover); display-m 64px Source Serif 400 (section title); h1 48px Manrope 600 (secondary title); h2 36px Manrope 600 (slide title, subsection); h3 28px Manrope 600 (card title); body-l 22px Manrope 400 (lede); body 18px Manrope 400 (default); body-s 15px Manrope 400 (caption); eyebrow 12-13px Manrope 700 + 0.16em tracking, uppercase. Serif is the editorial signature — reserve it for display moments: cover hero, section title, the one stat_callout number, KPI values, and pull-quotes. Content-slide titles and all body text use Manrope. Italic Source Serif is the accent treatment for pull-quotes. NEVER set serif on body text.

props.statement: Source Serif 4 for display moments only (cover/section/stat/KPI/quote); Manrope for titles+body+UI; JetBrains Mono for page numbers + small labels.

nodes.name: Cobalt Signal is the spine
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 1 (non-negotiable): Cobalt Signal is the spine. Covers and section dividers anchor on cobalt (or the dark ink poster). Content slides use cobalt as the one accent. If an accent colour starts to dominate, swap it for cobalt or ink. Separates a generic deck from a boardroom-ready one.

props.statement: Anchor covers/dividers on cobalt (or dark ink); content slides use cobalt as the single accent; demote any dominating accent back to cobalt/ink.

nodes.name: Every slide needs a visual element
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 2 (non-negotiable): Every slide needs a visual element — chart, big number, KPI tile, shape, or the brand-mark. Text-only slides are forgettable.

props.statement: No text-only slides; each slide carries a chart, big number, KPI tile, shape, or brand-mark.

nodes.name: One headline number per deck
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 3 (non-negotiable): One headline number per deck. Pick the single most important metric for the stat_callout (cobalt serif). Every other number lives in a kpi_grid, table, or chart. (Pitfall corollary: don't put more than one display-size number per slide.)

props.statement: Exactly one stat_callout headline number per deck (cobalt serif); all other numbers go in kpi_grid/table/chart; max one display-size number per slide.

nodes.name: Never use accent lines under titles
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 4 (non-negotiable): NEVER use accent lines under titles. The eyebrow + dash ABOVE the title IS the one accent line. Lines under titles are the hallmark of AI-generated decks.

props.statement: No accent/underline rule beneath titles; the eyebrow + dash above the title is the only accent line.

nodes.name: No gradient backgrounds on content
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 5 (non-negotiable): No gradient colour backgrounds on content. The only gradient in the system is the cover/closing orb, drawn automatically. Content surfaces are solid.

props.statement: Content surfaces are solid; the only gradient is the auto-drawn cover/closing orb.

nodes.name: Tabular numerals where numbers stack
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 6 (non-negotiable): Tabular numerals everywhere numbers stack. The tool sets this in both HTML and PPTX for value columns and KPI blocks.

props.statement: Use tabular (lining) numerals for stacked numbers — value columns and KPI blocks — set in both HTML and PPTX.

nodes.name: Captions matter — scope, period, interpretation
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 7 (non-negotiable): Captions matter. Every chart / KPI / stat slide ends with a one-line caption naming scope, period, and one interpretation.

props.statement: Every chart/KPI/stat slide ends with a one-line caption stating scope + period + one interpretation.

nodes.name: Palette must feel designed for THIS topic
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 8 (non-negotiable): The palette must feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping colours into a different deck would still 'work', the choice wasn't specific enough.

props.statement: Colour choices must be topic-specific; if the palette would work unchanged in a different deck, it is too generic.

nodes.name: Brand-mark in moderation
nodes.content: Discipline Rule 9 (non-negotiable): Brand-mark in moderation. Cover, section dividers, and closing get the brand-mark + wordmark; every other slide trusts the chrome (eyebrow + dash + page number + small mark) to carry the brand. Pitfall corollary: don't put the brand-mark on every content slide.

props.statement: Brand-mark + wordmark only on cover/section-dividers/closing; content slides carry brand via chrome (eyebrow + dash + page number + small mark).

nodes.name: Chart palette legend order — ≤6 series
nodes.content: When the tool assigns colours to chart series, the legend order is: 1. 254BB2 cobalt Signal (lead), 2. DF9B44 amber (supporting), 3. 2695AC cyan (supporting), 4. 913F82 magenta (accent), 5. 85B0FF light cobalt (accent), 6. 52657A slate (neutral/'rest'). Limit ≤6 series per chart — beyond that the palette runs out; split into multiple charts or use a stacked treatment.

props.statement: Chart series legend order: cobalt 254BB2 → amber DF9B44 → cyan 2695AC → magenta 913F82 → light-cobalt 85B0FF → slate 52657A; cap ≤6 series, else split or stack.

nodes.name: Background modes — paper/cream/signal/ink
nodes.content: Slide background modes. paper (default): white #FFFFFF fill, ink-900 text, cobalt eyebrow/dash accent — for content slides. cream: warm #FBF0E4 fill, ink-900 text, cobalt accent — for quotes/breathers. signal: cobalt #17368D fill, white text, AMBER accent — for section dividers / solid covers. ink: near-black #010204 fill, ink-100 text, AMBER accent — for covers, closings, dramatic charts. On dark backgrounds (signal/ink) the eyebrow + dash turn amber, chart axes + legends turn white, and the orb (cover/closing) appears.

props.statement: Four bg modes: paper(white,cobalt accent), cream(#FBF0E4,cobalt), signal(#17368D,amber,white text), ink(#010204,amber,ink-100 text). Dark bg → amber eyebrow/dash, white chart axes/legends, orb appears.

nodes.name: Sizing budget — 1920×1080 canvas + padding
nodes.content: Slide canvas is 1920 × 1080 px (16:9, matches PPTX widescreen exactly; everything inside overflow:hidden). Standard padding: 72px top, 96px sides, 120px bottom (room for the page number). Section gutter: 24–48px between major blocks. Card padding: 32px. Anything that doesn't fit in 1080px → split into two slides.

props.statement: Canvas 1920×1080 (16:9); padding 72 top / 96 sides / 120 bottom; section gutter 24–48px; card padding 32px; overflowing content splits to a second slide.

nodes.name: Don't repeat the same slide type
nodes.content: Common pitfall: Don't repeat the same slide type — kpi_grid × 5 reads as filler. Vary the rhythm: stat_callout → chart → kpi_grid → two_column → quote.

props.statement: Vary slide types (stat_callout → chart → kpi_grid → two_column → quote); repeating one type reads as filler.

nodes.name: Left-align body; center only display moments
nodes.content: Common pitfall: Don't center body text — left-align lists and ledes. Center only big-stat values, pull-quotes, and cover/closing titles.

props.statement: Left-align lists and ledes; center only big-stat values, pull-quotes, and cover/closing titles.

nodes.name: Abbreviate display numbers with K/M/B
nodes.content: Common pitfall: Don't dump 12-digit numbers — use K/M/B suffixes on display values. Tables and chart axes can stay precise.

props.statement: Use K/M/B suffixes on display values; tables and chart axes may stay precise.

nodes.name: Non-default brief overrides — monochrome / all-dark / external brand
nodes.content: The tool defaults to the RA Signal system. Non-default user briefs: 'Make it monochrome' → drop the amber/cyan/magenta accents; use cobalt + ink only; keep every slide on paper and avoid multi-series charts that would pull in accent colours. 'Go all-dark' → set bg:ink on more slides; charts auto-switch to white axes; keep contrast in mind for captions. 'Match {external brand}' → larger change; ask for the primary hex; a one-off deck can use it as the accent, but the RA brand-mark + wordmark should be removed for a non-RA deliverable.

props.statement: Brief overrides: monochrome=cobalt+ink only on paper, no multi-series; all-dark=more bg:ink, white axes; match-external-brand=ask for primary hex, use as accent, strip RA brand-mark+wordmark.

nodes.name: Source Serif 4 font
nodes.content: Source Serif 4 (serif) — the RA editorial display typeface. Used for cover hero, section titles, the one stat_callout number, KPI values, and pull-quotes (italic for pull-quotes). Never on body text.

props.purpose: Display/serif typeface for cover hero, section titles, stat/KPI numbers, pull-quotes.

nodes.name: Manrope font
nodes.content: Manrope (sans) — the RA body/UI typeface. Used for titles, body, labels, and UI. h1 48 / h2 36 / h3 28 weights at 600; body 22/18/15 at 400; eyebrow 12-13 at 700 + 0.16em tracking uppercase.

props.purpose: Sans body/UI typeface for titles, body, labels, eyebrows.

nodes.name: JetBrains Mono font
nodes.content: JetBrains Mono — the RA monospace typeface for page numbers and small data labels.

props.purpose: Monospace typeface for page numbers and small data labels.

nodes.name: RA brand-mark + wordmark
nodes.content: Resonance Analytics brand-mark and wordmark. Placed only on cover, section dividers, and closing slides; content slides carry the brand via chrome (eyebrow + dash + page number + small mark). For a non-RA 'match external brand' deliverable the brand-mark + wordmark should be removed.

nodes.name: compose-pptx-skill
nodes.content: The compose-pptx skill directory, symlinked as /skills/compose-pptx/. Source: hackathon-skills/compose-pptx/. Contents: SKILL.md, spec_reference.md, design.md, design_system_deck_example.html, tokens.css, templates/README.md (where to drop ra_template.pptx), assets/ (brand assets for building the template). The agent calling compose_deck must be able to read_file('/skills/compose-pptx/...') — its skills= list must include /skills/ or /skills/compose-pptx/.

props.purpose: compose_deck deck-authoring skill: SKILL.md, spec_reference.md, design.md, tokens.css, design_system_deck_example.html, templates/, assets/

nodes.name: compose_deck template mode
nodes.content:
compose_deck 'template mode': when ra_template.pptx ships in templates/, template mode is ON by default — the renderer paints ONLY content; the template's master + layouts supply all chrome (backgrounds, the cobalt-orb cover, the RA logo lockup/wordmark, footers, page numbers) and the embedded brand fonts. The renderer looks up exactly seven layouts BY NAME and routes slides by type + bg: RA_TitleSlide (cover, slide 1 — fills title idx 10 + subtitle idx 1), RA_thankyou (the closing slide — title idx 11 + subtitle idx 1), RA_blank (every light content slide / default, pure white #FFFFFF), RA_cream (content with bg:'cream', warm cream #FBF0E4 — quotes/data-notes), RA_dark (content with bg:'ink'/'charcoal'/'dark', near-black), RA_SectionSignal (section_divider with bg:'signal', solid cobalt #17368D), RA_SectionWhite (section_divider with bg:'paper'/'white', subtle blue #F9FAFC paper). Placeholders idx 10/11/1 are the renderer's HARD CONTRACT — the builder re-stamps them and deletes the stock Date(10)/Footer(11)/SlideNumber(12) placeholders that would collide; content/section layouts carry ZERO content placeholders (the renderer paints everything and strips empties). Known limitations: (1) page numbers show current-slide only (e.g. '7'), not '07 / 13' — cosmetic, a PowerPoint slidenum field can't render a deck total; (2) bg:'signal' is section-divider ONLY — a CONTENT slide with bg:'signal' routes to RA_blank (white) and gets white text painted on it; use bg:'ink' (→ RA_dark) for dark CONTENT; bg:'cream' now routes to its own RA_cream (no longer renders on white) and RA_SectionWhite carries #F9FAFC paper; (3) cover + closing use the top-left logo lockup with NO footer lockup (by design, matching the from-scratch hero treatment); content/section slides carry the lockup in the footer; (4) fonts are EMBEDDED (Source Serif 4 / Manrope / JetBrains Mono, full faces, SIL OFL) via embed_fonts.py and survive the python-pptx load+save so rendered decks inherit them; (5) canvas is Inches(13.333) = 12,191,695 EMU to match compose_deck's SLIDE_W_IN=13.333 exactly. Verified: built + smoke-tested end-to-end through the real compose_deck._render_pptx (a 14-slide deck exercising every slide type incl. bg:'cream'), rasterised (LibreOffice → pdftoppm), 4-lens adversarial visual QA (brand/typography/logo/layout) — zero confirmed defects; background pixels sampled exact (RA_blank #FFFFFF, RA_SectionWhite #F9FAFC, RA_cream #FBF0E4); XML audit confirms 9 font parts + embedTrueTypeFonts.

compose_deck template mode: drop ra_template.pptx into hackathon-orchestrator/skills/compose-pptx/templates/ (per templates/README.md). When the template file is present the tool logs '[compose_deck] template mode ON' and the renderer paints content over the template chrome. When absent it draws the cobalt-orb cover and brand-mark natively. Confirm in the free-edition smoke test that the .pptx opens with editable charts and the preview renders the cobalt-orb cover.

props.statement: Place ra_template.pptx in skills/compose-pptx/templates/ to enable template mode; tool logs '[compose_deck] template mode ON' only when the template is present.

nodes.name: decks route through compose_deck
nodes.content: When the user asks for slides / a deck / a presentation / a PowerPoint, route to compose_deck (NOT compose_document). Call compose_deck(title=..., deck_spec=[...]) — one JSON spec per the compose-pptx skill. It renders an editable .pptx with NATIVE charts + an HTML preview and returns preview_url + pptx_url; surface BOTH. Other binary office docs (docx / xlsx / csv / pdf) stay on compose_document, never run_python_code. In subagents/prompts.py: replace the ~line-452 'everything office-format → compose_document' bullet, and in the ~line-601 keyword-routing list point 'deck / slides / powerpoint / presentation' at compose_deck while leaving 'word doc / excel / spreadsheet / csv / pdf' on compose_document.

props.statement: deck / slides / presentation / PowerPoint → compose_deck; word doc / excel / spreadsheet / csv / pdf → compose_document. Surface both preview_url and pptx_url.

nodes.name: compose_deck wiring into orchestrator
nodes.content: compose_deck is wired into the orchestrator and data_viz agents as an action tool. Call it with a deck_spec (list of typed slide dicts) to render a PPTX + HTML preview; the tool loads its own template and fonts internally — no skill files involved. See the Tool:compose_deck page for the signature and the SlideType / DeckGuide pages for the spec.

props.statement: Wire compose_deck via 5 edits to deploy_orchestrator_agent.py (import, ALLOWED_TOOLS, factory instantiate, tool-list, skill scope) + bump python-pptx>=1.0.0.

nodes.name: compose_deck node decks API route
nodes.content: compose_deck returns …/api/decks/<id> (HTML preview, inline) and …/api/decks/<id>.pptx (download). Add a dedicated server/src/routes/decks.ts mirroring documents.ts: it lists the shared documents folder (/Volumes/workspace/ai_ops/agent_scratch/documents) via /api/2.0/fs/directories, matches the file whose name starts with `${id}__` and ends `.${ext}`, fetches via /api/2.0/fs/files. GET /api/decks/:id.pptx sets Content-Type application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation + Content-Disposition attachment; GET /api/decks/:id serves text/html inline. Register in index.ts next to the documents route. A dedicated /api/decks route is recommended because the existing /api/documents/:id proxy picks ONE file per id and won't distinguish preview-vs-pptx, even though the deck .pptx/.html/.json all land in the same documents/ folder.

props.statement: Add server/src/routes/decks.ts (GET /api/decks/:id → HTML preview, GET /api/decks/:id.pptx → pptx download) matching `${id}__` files in the shared documents folder; register in index.ts.

nodes.name: build_ra_template.py
nodes.content:
Reproducible builder script (next to ra_template.pptx in templates/) that rewrites ra_template.pptx via python-pptx, embedding the brand assets in ../assets/ and re-stamping the idx 10/11/1 placeholders while deleting the stock Date(10)/Footer(11)/SlideNumber(12) placeholders python-pptx ships. It re-builds the embedded-font parts + <p:embeddedFontLst>. It is the source of truth to regenerate the template after an asset, layout, or font tweak: `cd hackathon-skills/compose-pptx/templates && python3 build_ra_template.py`. Calls embed_fonts.py to embed the brand fonts.

Builder script that generates ra_template.pptx — embeds the brand PNGs (cover poster, wordmarks, geo-mark, ra-lockup) into the slide layouts and places ra-lockup-{dark,light}.png wherever the old cobalt-square wordmark sat. To regenerate after editing the SVGs, re-run the rsvg-trim + PIL-compose steps (see the build guide).

props.purpose: reproducibly rebuild ra_template.pptx (layouts, placeholders, embedded fonts)

nodes.name: embed_fonts.py
nodes.content:
Helper script (in templates/) that embeds the brand fonts (Source Serif 4 / Manrope / JetBrains Mono, full faces, all SIL OFL) from ../assets/fonts/ into a .pptx so the serif + mono render even on machines without them installed. Called by build_ra_template.py, but also runs standalone on any .pptx: `embed_fonts.py ra_template.pptx`. Re-run after hand-editing the template in PowerPoint (which may drop the embedded fonts).

Script that embeds the bundled static TTFs into ra_template.pptx so the serif + mono render on machines without them installed. Fonts are embedded full (not subset), ~1.6 MB on disk.

nodes.content: Asset format rule of thumb: vector marks (brand-mark, geo-mark) ship as SVG + PNG twin (crisp and recolorable via Convert to Shape). Gradient/blur backgrounds (the cover orb) ship as high-res PNG because CSS radial gradients + blur don't round-trip to SVG cleanly. (2) Eyebrow + dash — Manrope 700, 13px, 0.16em tracking, UPPERCASE, cobalt on light / amber on dark, with a 28x2px dash to its left. (3) Corner mark — the top-right 'RA · 06 · TYPE' label, JetBrains Mono, 11px, 0.12em tracking, muted.

props.statement: Ghost section numeral (Source Serif 4, white ~8-10%, ~520pt, top-right), eyebrow+dash (Manrope 700, 13px, 0.16em, UPPERCASE, cobalt/amber, 28x2px dash), and corner mark (JetBrains Mono, 11px, 0.12em, muted) are recreated as type — no asset file.

nodes.name: bar_top_hosts
nodes.content: Top-N district/state leaderboard bar chart. DATA SHAPE: district/state × value, sorted desc, top-N. WHEN: top-N leaderboards (districts by facility count, states by coverage), e.g. top-10 districts by facility count. Inside the render_chart 'bar' allow-list; zero-baseline per data-journalism skill. Filter sentinel/unknown district codes.

props.when_to_use: Top-N district/state leaderboards (e.g. by facility count).

nodes.name: line_facility_type_trend
nodes.content: Multi-category line chart across an ordered axis. DATA SHAPE: ordered key × value, one line per category (e.g. facility-type counts across district tertiles). WHEN: show how multiple categories evolve across an ordered axis. Inside the render_chart 'line' allow-list.

props.data_shape: ordered key × value × category (e.g. facility type)
props.when_to_use: Multi-category trend across an ordered axis.

nodes.name: heatmap_origin_year
nodes.content: District × indicator intensity heatmap. DATA SHAPE: top-15 districts (rows) × indicators (cols) × intensity (cell). WHEN: show which districts are weakest on which indicators. Inside the render_chart 'heatmap' allow-list.

props.when_to_use: Which districts are weakest on which indicators.

nodes.name: donut_population_mix
nodes.content: Facility-type composition donut. DATA SHAPE: ≤5 facility-type slices for one cohort. WHEN: composition of facility types (donut beats pie per data-journalism skill, ≤5 slices). Inside the render_chart 'donut' allow-list.

props.when_to_use: Facility-type composition with ≤5 slices (donut over pie).

nodes.name: bar_stacked_district
nodes.content: Stacked-bar trend for one district/state across multiple categories. DATA SHAPE: ordered key × stacked categories for a single district/state (e.g. facility-type mix across district tertiles). WHEN: one-entity multi-category breakdown. Inside the render_chart 'bar' allow-list.

props.data_shape: ordered key × stacked categories, single entity
props.when_to_use: One-district/state multi-category breakdown.

nodes.name: bar_acceptance_rates
nodes.content: Percentage bar chart with explicit value labels. DATA SHAPE: category × percent, with value labels. WHEN: render coverage/indicator rates as percentages with explicit labels. Inside the render_chart 'bar' allow-list. Computes the rate from the NFHS-5 indicators / facility counts.

props.when_to_use: Render coverage/indicator rates as labelled percentages.

nodes.name: dumbbell_rank_reversal
nodes.content: Side-by-side ranked bars or dumbbell connecting each district's raw health-burden rank to its urbanisation-adjusted rank, surfacing the reversal. Data shape: per district {iso, name, raw, adjusted}. When-to-use: the urbanisation confound — show that the supply-vs-need ranking flips once you control for urbanisation (R-confounder-urbanisation). Pitfall: always label which metric each column is.

props.when_to_use: show a rank reversal between two metrics (raw vs urbanisation-adjusted)

nodes.name: lorenz_curve
nodes.content: Lorenz curve (cumulative share of districts vs cumulative share of facilities) with a 45-degree equality line, annotated with the Gini coefficient. Data shape: paired lorenz_x / lorenz_y arrays plus gini. When-to-use: facility-coverage concentration — a few districts hold most facilities while ~245 have zero. Pitfall: log or rank the skew; never a linear histogram.

props.when_to_use: show facility-coverage concentration / inequality (Gini)

nodes.name: gdp_burden_bubble
nodes.content: Log-log bubble scatter of an urbanisation proxy (x) vs facility count per district (y), bubble size = sample/survey women, color = region, with an OLS trend line and r/R²/slope annotation. Data shape: points {iso, name, region, gdp, per1000, hosted, note} (the field names gdp/per1000/hosted are the stable data-shape contract keys — map urban-proxy/facility/sample onto them). When-to-use: supply-vs-need — show the raw relationship AND the honest null once urbanisation is controlled (R-confounder-urbanisation).

props.data_shape: points {iso, name, region, gdp, per1000, hosted}
props.when_to_use: show a correlation with regional grouping and outlier callouts (supply vs need, urbanisation-confounded)

nodes.name: iceberg_waterline
nodes.content: Stacked single-bar 'waterline' splitting a district total into 'has at least one recorded facility' (above the line) and 'zero recorded facilities in the sample' (below), with a reframe toggle. Data shape: {total, abroad, inside} kept as the stable contract keys — map them as: total=all districts, abroad=districts with >=1 facility, inside=zero-facility districts (the headline-vs-hidden split). When-to-use: The Iceberg — show that a large share of districts have no recorded facility behind the headline facility count. Pitfall: proportional ink — segment width must equal the share exactly; remember it is a ~10k SAMPLE (coverage, not census).

props.data_shape: {total, abroad, inside} mapped to all/has-facility/zero-facility district counts
props.when_to_use: reframe a headline count as a share of a larger hidden total (iceberg)

nodes.name: kpi
nodes.content: KPI tile — single headline number, e.g. zero-facility district count (~245). Renders perfectly for the medical-desert demos.

props.when_to_use: Single headline number (e.g. zero-facility district count).

nodes.name: design-system
nodes.content: Design System domain — chart styling + selection guidance: the editorial palette, typography, chart-selection matrix, colour-usage rules, accessibility. Route when the user asks what chart to use, how to visualize, palette / branding / styling questions. The knowledge lives in this graph as DesignRule, ChartType and ChartRecipe nodes (STYLED_BY / VISUALIZED_BY / USES_CHART edges) — ask find_skill for the specific chart or design topic; no files.

props.summary: Design System skill: chart styling + guidance (palette, typography, chart-selection matrix, color rules).
